rulings.Case Title: SIDNEY UNOBSKEY VS. SIMON MICHAEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 26, 2021 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT GARY MILLAR, GAIL SABIN MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES is continued to August 18, 2021 per stipulation and order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PRIYA SANGER ET AL VS. AHE AHN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 26, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT AHE AHN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Judgment is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that there was not transfer of asset as a matter of law. Schoenfeld v. Norberg (1970) 11 Cal.App.3d 755 is not on point as expressly stated in this action by judge Spero in October 15, 2029 Order. The Court notes that this order was not discussed and was not provided by the moving party. Moving party is further order to file no later than 9:00 a.m. on July 26, 2021 its reply, which appears to have been served on Plaintiffs (with courtesy copy provided to the court), but not submitted for filing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA MUNMON VS. SANDY GAIL MUNMON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 26, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT SANDY MUNMON MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Hearing Required. By no later than 9:00 a.m. on July 26, 2021 Defendant to file and provide a courtesy copy of complete verified responses without objections to Special Interrogatories, Set One; Requests for Production Set One and provide a date certain for a deposition in the month of August 2021. If ordered responses are not provided, the Court will deny the motion. If complete verified responses without objections and a date for deposition is provided, the Court will consider granting the motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
